name: Tordomar — circular from Lerma
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Lerma offer a diverse landscape in the province of Burgos, Spain. The region features the Arlanza River Valley with its fertile plains and historic viewpoints, alongside the unique juniper forests of the Sabinares del Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park. Cyclists can expect varied terrain, from well-paved roads suitable for road bikes to routes with significant elevation gains, providing a mix of challenges and scenic beauty. The area's historic town centers and natural features enhance the cycling experience. Best touring cycling routes around Lerma The most popular touring cycling route is Tordomar — circular from Lerma, an easy 13.0 miles (21.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 22 minutes to complete, offering a gentle ride through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Fontioso Church – Los Arcos Viewpoint loop from Lerma, a difficult 30.0 miles (48.3 km) path. This route features a substantial elevation gain of 769 meters and takes approximately 4 hours 35 minutes to complete. Local touring cyclists also love the Alto de Peña Alta (1078 m) – Statue of El Cid in Mecerreyes loop from Quintanilla del Agua y Tordueles, a 50.5 miles (81.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain with over 1,000 meters of ascent, often completed in about 5 hours 14 minutes. Touring cycling around Lerma is defined by the Arlanza River Valley, the juniper forests of Sabinares del Arlanza, and the dramatic La Yecla gorge. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Lerma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 3 reviews. More than 70 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Lerma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Lerma?
A: There are over a dozen touring cycling routes around Lerma, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. You can find routes ranging from easy, flat rides to more challenging paths with significant elevation gains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes around Lerma?
A: The terrain around Lerma is diverse. Many routes feature well-paved surfaces, ideal for touring bikes, especially those exploring the Arlanza River Valley. However, some routes, like the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontioso Church – Los Arcos Viewpoint loop from Lerma&lt;/a&gt;, include substantial elevation gains, requiring good fitness. You'll encounter a mix of open plains, river valleys, and more undulating landscapes as you venture towards natural parks.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, Lerma offers several easy touring cycling routes. For a gentle introduction, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030263961/tordomar-circular-from-lerma" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tordomar — circular from Lerma&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.0 miles (21.0 km) long with minimal elevation. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Arcos Viewpoint loop from Quintanilla del Agua&lt;/a&gt;, which is also relatively flat and suitable for a relaxed ride.

Q: What historical sites or viewpoints can I visit along the cycling routes?
A: Lerma's touring cycling routes often pass by or offer access to significant historical and natural attractions. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5698252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza Mayor and Ducal Palace of Lerma&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5517941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Arcos&lt;/a&gt; for panoramic views of the Arlanza River Valley. Some routes might also lead you near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5454929" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Statue of El Cid in Mecerreyes&lt;/a&gt; or the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5698240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of Saint Peter the Apostle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I explore natural parks or gorges on a touring bike near Lerma?
A: Absolutely. The region is home to the Sabinares del Arlanza - La Yecla Natural Park, known for its unique juniper forests and the dramatic La Yecla gorge. While some parts might be more challenging, longer touring routes like the 'Parque Natural Sabinares del Arlanza - La Yecla' (129 km) are designed to take you through these stunning natural landscapes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4127823" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mataviejas Gorge&lt;/a&gt; is also a notable natural feature in the area.

Q: Is it possible to find circular touring cycling routes in Lerma?
A: Yes, many of the touring cycling routes around Lerma are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and end your journey in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030263961/tordomar-circular-from-lerma" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tordomar — circular from Lerma&lt;/a&gt; and the more demanding &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20149781" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de Peña Alta (1078 m) – Statue of El Cid in Mecerreyes loop from Quintanilla del Agua y Tordueles&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Lerma?
A: The spring and autumn months are considered ideal for touring cycling in Lerma. During these seasons, temperatures are mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be hot, and winters can be cold, so spring and autumn offer the best balance for outdoor activities.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about cycling in Lerma?
A: The touring cycling routes in Lerma are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars from more than 3 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the picturesque scenery of the Arlanza River Valley, and the opportunity to combine cycling with exploring historical sites.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Lerma offers several difficult routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40011012" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontioso Church – Los Arcos Viewpoint loop from Lerma&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 30.0 miles (48.3 km) path with a substantial elevation gain of 769 meters. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38161292" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Arcos Viewpoint loop from Urbanización San Antón&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 37.2 km with nearly 500 meters of ascent.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Lerma?
A: Lerma, being a historic town, offers various parking options. You can typically find parking in or near the town center, especially around the Plaza Mayor, which can serve as a convenient starting point for many routes. Specific parking details for individual routes might vary, so it's advisable to check local information upon arrival.

Q: Are there options for public transport to access cycling routes in Lerma?
A: Lerma is accessible by public transport, primarily bus services connecting it to larger cities like Burgos. While buses can transport you to Lerma, carrying bicycles on public transport might depend on the specific bus operator and available space. It's recommended to check with the local bus companies regarding their policies for transporting bikes before planning your trip.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling in natural areas like Sabinares del Arlanza?
A: Generally, touring cycling on established roads and trails within natural parks like Sabinares del Arlanza does not require special permits. However, it's always good practice to respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and be mindful of protected areas. For specific activities or off-road cycling, it's advisable to consult the official park authority for any updated permit requirements.

Q: Can I find bike rental services in Lerma?
A: While specific bike rental shops in Lerma are not detailed in the provided information, as a popular cycling destination, it is likely that local tourism offices or specialized sports shops in Lerma or nearby larger towns may offer bike rental services. It's recommended to inquire locally or search online for current rental options before your visit.
tips: Great views of the entire valley and the Arlanza River. Strolling through Lerma and not going to the Los Arcos viewpoint would be unforgivable. Dating from the early 18th century, it was built to unite the Collegiate Church of San Pedro Apóstol and the Palace of the Duke of Lerma.
Roman bridge over the Arlanza River in Tordómar is like an entrance to this beautiful town and an authentic symbol of the town.

It was consecrated in 1617 and depended directly on Rome, which is why the papal emblem can be seen in many places (the Duke of Lerma got it). Beautiful temple, in which the altarpiece and the praying sculpture of Don Cristóbal de Rojas stand out.

waypoints.name: Collegiate Church of Saint Peter the Apostle
